text: The surgeon performs a robotic partial nephrectomy on a solitary right kidney with multiple renal masses. The kidney is positioned at a sixty-degree right lobotomy angle, and a five-port robotic system is utilized. The main renal vessels are dissected circumferentially for bulldog clamp placement. Girota's fascia is opened away from the tumor, and dissection proceeds along the renal capsule until the mass is exposed. Fat is cleared circumferentially around the mass, revealing one to two centimeters of normal parenchyma for future reconstruction.

The surgeon scores the margins of the Reynolds capsule with electrocautery to delineate the resection boundaries. Two laparoscopic bulldog clamps are applied for Hiller clamping. Tumors are resected along the scored margins using cold scissors, while the bedside assistant uses suction to clear the resection bed and applies slight counter-retraction for improved visualization.

The surgeon performs renography in two layers using robotic needle drivers. A 20 cm, 2-0 valve suture on an SH-I needle is used as a running suture to secure larger vessels and entries into the collecting system at the tumor's excision bed. The renal capsule is re-approximated with a continuous horizontal matrix suture on a CT-1 needle, with sliding hemlock clips applied after each pass. After renography, the hilum is unclamped, and the renal excision bed is inspected for hemostasis. The defect is covered with fluocele and surgicel.
